CREATE TABLE test_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (category VARCHAR(255), score DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO additional_data (category, score) VALUES ('A', NULL);
SELECT category, sys.median_avg(score) FROM additional_data GROUP BY category;
SELECT sys.median_avg(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE value >= 2.0) AS filtered_data;
CREATE TABLE join_data (key_id INT, ref_value DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO join_data (key_id, ref_value) VALUES (3, NULL);
SELECT sys.median_avg(j.ref_value) FROM join_data j JOIN test_data t ON j.key_id = t.id WHERE t.value < 5.0;
SELECT sys.median_avg(COALESCE(t.value, j.ref_value)) FROM test_data t FULL OUTER JOIN join_data j ON t.id = j.key_id;
SELECT sys.median_avg(value) FROM test_data WHERE value > 10.0;
